title: Q2 bookings pace 5% ahead of target through April
anchor: $880K closed-won versus $840K plan-pacing target through April 24.
connect: Q2 weighted pipeline sits at $1.6M versus the $1.4M commit.
body: $880K closed-won versus $840K plan-pacing target through April 24. Q2 weighted pipeline sits at $1.6M versus the $1.4M commit.
trace: Q2 actual bookings $880K versus plan-pacing target $840K shows 5% ahead. Weighted pipeline provides $200K buffer above commit.

title: Enterprise win rate jumps to 31% in Q2
anchor: 8 of 26 enterprise deals closed-won this quarter, average amount $187K.
connect: Trailing four quarters ran at 22% win rate across 180 deals.
body: 8 of 26 enterprise deals closed-won this quarter, average amount $187K. Trailing four quarters ran at 22% win rate across 180 deals.
trace: Q2 enterprise win rate 30.77% significantly above trailing four quarters at 22.22%. Deal size also higher.

title: Q3 enterprise pipeline coverage sits at 4.1x
anchor: $4.9M open pipeline across 22 deals versus $1.2M enterprise plan.
connect: 22 deals averages $224K each in the enterprise queue.
body: $4.9M open pipeline across 22 deals versus $1.2M enterprise plan. 22 deals averages $224K each in the enterprise queue.
trace: Q3 enterprise coverage at 4.10x ($4.92M pipeline / $1.2M plan) shows healthy coverage above standard thresholds.

title: Marketing sources 84% of Q2 net new pipeline
anchor: $2.5M of $3.0M total pipeline created carries marketing attribution.
connect: Marketing-sourced enterprise deals win at 29% versus outbound at 14%.
body: $2.5M of $3.0M total pipeline created carries marketing attribution. Marketing-sourced enterprise deals win at 29% versus outbound at 14%.
trace: Marketing-sourced share at 83.9% of Q2 pipeline creation. Win rate differential shows 2x performance advantage for marketing-sourced enterprise deals.

title: Beacon Systems head-to-head record flips to 5W-1L
anchor: 6 competitive deals this quarter versus 6 in Q1.
connect: Q1 record sat at 3W-3L versus the same competitor.
body: 6 competitive deals this quarter versus 6 in Q1. Q1 record sat at 3W-3L versus the same competitor.
trace: Head-to-head versus Beacon Systems improved from 50% win rate (Q1) to 83% win rate (Q2).

title: Q3 commit builds $150K buffer above current pipeline
connect: Q2 weighted pipeline runs $200K above commit at the same confidence.
body: $1.75M weighted at 80% confidence versus $1.6M commit. Q2 weighted pipeline runs $200K above commit at the same confidence.
trace: Q3 weighted pipeline $1.75M provides $150K buffer above $1.6M commit, similar to Q2 pattern.

title: Mid-market renewals pace at 112% NRR through Q2
connect: Q1 mid-market NRR ran at 110% across 8 renewals.
body: 9 renewals totaling $620K versus $540K in Q1. Q1 mid-market NRR ran at 110% across 8 renewals.
trace: Q2 mid-market NRR at 1.12 shows slight improvement from Q1 at 1.1, with higher renewal volume.

title: Enterprise deal size jumps $42K quarter-over-quarter
anchor: Q2 enterprise wins average $187K versus Q1 at $145K.
connect: Q2 enterprise pipeline averages $224K per open deal.
body: Q2 enterprise wins average $187K versus Q1 at $145K. Q2 enterprise pipeline averages $224K per open deal.
trace: Enterprise ASP improvement from $145K (Q1) to $187K (Q2) with Q3 pipeline showing continued size growth.

title: Close-date slips total $180K this week
anchor: 4 deals moved from Q2 to Q3 between April 20-26.
connect: 3 enterprise deals sit in procurement with $617K total amount.
body: 4 deals moved from Q2 to Q3 between April 20-26. 3 enterprise deals sit in procurement with $617K total amount.
trace: Weekly close-date movement of $180K across 4 deals, with separate procurement queue holding larger amounts.

title: Mid-market creation pace holds at 42 deals monthly
anchor: 42 opportunities created March 25 through April 24, total $1.9M.
connect: Average mid-market ACV runs at $45K per opportunity.
body: 42 opportunities created March 25 through April 24, total $1.9M. Average mid-market ACV runs at $45K per opportunity.
trace: 30-day mid-market pipeline creation at 42 deals with $45,180 average ACV shows consistent generation.

title: Expansion opportunities surface $340K in 30 days
anchor: 8 expansion deals from customer health reviews, average $42.5K each.
connect: Customer Success pipeline feeds from existing account base.
body: 8 expansion deals from customer health reviews, average $42.5K each. Customer Success pipeline feeds from existing account base.
trace: 30-day expansion pipeline creation of $340K across 8 opportunities sourced from customer health reviews.

title: Q2 net new pipeline totals $3M across 70 opportunities
anchor: Pipeline creation spans enterprise and mid-market segments.
connect: Average opportunity size runs at $43K across all segments.
body: Pipeline creation spans enterprise and mid-market segments. Average opportunity size runs at $43K across all segments.
trace: Q2 net new pipeline $3,003,132 across 70 deals shows $42,902 average opportunity size.

title: Enterprise pipeline concentrates in 22 Q3 opportunities
connect: Enterprise plan needs $1.2M from this pipeline base.
body: $4.9M total amount averaging $224K per deal. Enterprise plan needs $1.2M from this pipeline base.
trace: Q3 enterprise pipeline concentration in 22 deals with high average deal size supports coverage ratio.

title: Outbound enterprise conversion trails marketing by half
anchor: Outbound enterprise win rate at 14% versus marketing-sourced at 29%.
connect: Marketing attribution covers 84% of total pipeline creation.
body: Outbound enterprise win rate at 14% versus marketing-sourced at 29%. Marketing attribution covers 84% of total pipeline creation.
trace: Enterprise win rate gap between outbound (14.3%) and marketing-sourced (28.6%) shows channel performance differential.

title: Procurement queue holds $617K across 3 enterprise deals
anchor: Deals cleared signoff and contract revisions, awaiting final approval.
connect: Average procurement deal size runs at $206K.
body: Deals cleared signoff and contract revisions, awaiting final approval. Average procurement deal size runs at $206K.
trace: 3 enterprise deals totaling $617,117 in final procurement stage, average $205,706 each.

title: Q1 final commit landed at $1.3M versus $4.6M plan
anchor: Q1 commit represents 28% of total quarterly plan.
connect: Q2 commit at $1.4M versus $5M plan runs similar percentage.
body: Q1 commit represents 28% of total quarterly plan. Q2 commit at $1.4M versus $5M plan runs similar percentage.
trace: Q1 commit-to-plan ratio at 28% ($1.3M/$4.6M) consistent with Q2 at 28% ($1.4M/$5M).
